equivalence_check: watch_form == syscall_form EXACTLY. This is the load-bearing assertion of scenario 12: the two equivalent forms MUST produce identical cost. typical each: 2000*1200/1e9 = 0.0024 GB/day -> 0.0024*30.4*5.00 = $0.36/mo. Standardized from 900 to 1200 B/event (locked 2026-06-03).
math: Each form: file-watch event ~1200 B. typical 2000*1200/1e9 = 0.0024 GB/day -> $0.36/mo. The forms are equivalent so each costs the SAME; a ruleset listing both (as here, to fixture the equivalence) sums to 2x only because both are loaded. Standardized from 900 to 1200 B/event (locked 2026-06-03).

naive_wrong_model.comment: A naive impl that treats the -w watch as a single-path 'one rule' but expands the -F path syscall form into its full perm-group syscall list (or vice versa) prices the two forms DIFFERENTLY.
naive_wrong_model.error: watch_form != syscall_form. The correct model recognizes the desugaring (f3 section 2.2) and prices both identically. Any nonzero delta between the two forms is the bug this scenario catches.
